mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS < 2.0% are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-test p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 net new jobs, signaling weakening labor demand that precedes earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100,000 persons for two consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following sustained PAYEMS growth < 100k/month are not significantly negative (mean return > -0.5% or t-test p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously, indicating weakening consumer demand and labor market stress that compress corporate profit margins and drive negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp within 1 month
regime_hint: demand contraction with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 3% year-over-year growth, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains that support earnings expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of INDPRO YoY growth > 3% are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.0% or t-test p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,100,000 units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2% annualized, indicating a demand-side slowdown in both residential investment and overall economic activity that portends negative equity returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,100,000 units AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 3.0%, indicating real rates are negative and the Fed is behind the inflation curve, creating policy uncertainty that drives negative equity returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate aggressive future tightening.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation surprise
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines below 3.8% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200,000 net new jobs per month, signaling a tight labor market with strong wage pressure that increases corporate labor costs and compresses profit margins, driving negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 3.8% AND PAYEMS monthly change > 200,000 for two consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates from prior 3-month trend by more than 1.5 percentage points, signaling a sharp loss of consumer momentum that precedes earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next 5 trading days as investors front-run consensus revisions.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth rate declines > 1.5pp from prior 3-month average
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.07 over any 1-year rolling window.
